title: qemu machine disappears after upgrade to 5.0.0
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issueUpgrading from podman 4.9.3 to 5.0.0 the qemu machine has just disappeared from podman's radar.
The files are still there, but a podman machine list does not list it.
I must have missed some warning during the install - but I can not find any source of documentation about the change altogether.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. on macos, upgrade podman from 4.9.3 to 5.0.0 - having a qemu machine


### Describe the results you received

The podman machine is gone - I have to re-initialize a machine

### Describe the results you expected

The existing qemu machine is still listed. OR - during the upgrade I am proposed to entirely remove it (because it occupies ~60GiB)

comments.body:
Maintainers mentioned this in the [v5.0.0 Release Note](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.0.0):

> QEMU is no longer a supported VM provider for podman machine on Mac. Instead, the native Apple hypervisor is supported.
@bc-lee would you also have a link to the documentation explaining how to reclaim the GiB occupied by the qemu VM, eventually migrating the volumes from that format to the new, please?

https://blog.podman.io/2024/03/migration-of-podman-4-to-podman-5-machines/ might be helpful.

if i was in your position, i would do:

1. `podman machine reset`
2. `rm -fr ~/.config/containers/podman/machine/`
3. `rm -fr~/.local/share/containers/podman/machine`
4. `podman system connection ls`
5. identify any system connections associated with podman machine
6. `podman system connection rm <connection_name>`
7. Rm any unneeded SSH keys in ~/.ssh

that should get you very close
Moving it to a discussion because this is expected
